title: agent-sandbox Workload
description: The parameterization that replaces 'install claude'. A Workload record is handed to `agent-sandbox run`: the launcher selects a runtime via the backend, allocates a subnet, brings up the name-level-allowlist firewall, waits for its health, then execs the workload's entrypoint. No workload-specific logic lives in the library.

image.description: Container image the workload runs (the workload payload, e.g. an agent). Distinct from the firewall image, which the library owns.

entrypoint.description: argv the sandbox execs once the firewall is healthy. NO 'install claude' here — the workload brings its own command.

env.description: name -> value map exported into the workload container.

user.description: uid or name the workload runs as. Must be unprivileged; the workload container drops ALL capabilities and runs read-only.

workspace_mount.description: Host path bound to the container's /workspace. Omitted when seed_from_git is set (the workspace is seeded from git into a volume instead of bound).

egress_allowlist.description: Allowed destinations as HOSTNAMES, never IPs. Enforced at the forward proxy (squid CONNECT host check) + a pinned static DNS resolver + an iptables ipset backstop — one mechanism. An IP allowlist is a security regression (CDN over/under-block, rotating IPs, resolver/DoH bypass) AND breaks the tamper-evident egress log.

ephemeral.description: When true, the workload's volumes are throwaway and torn down on exit; teardown fails loud on any survivor (the guarantee is verified, not assumed).

seed_from_git.description: worktree-seed: seed the workspace from a git ref inside the sandbox; the workload's writes are extracted onto review_branch on the host, never onto the host working tree.

ref.description: The git ref (commit/branch/tag) the sandbox workspace is seeded from.

review_branch.description: The host branch the workload's committed writes land on for review.

backend.description: Runtime backend seam. 'local' runs the Kata->gVisor->runc auto-downgrade ladder on the local Docker engine. 'hosted' (a managed remote sandbox) is a documented interface stub. Every backend MUST enforce the allowlist at a forward proxy and keep the proxy log as the egress log.
